metadata.title: Awards, Honours & Persons in News Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Who received the Indian Farmers Fertiliser Cooperative Limited (IFFCO) Sahitya Samman 2025 for outstanding contribution to Hindi literature?
questions.explanation: Indian Farmers Fertiliser Cooperative Limited (IFFCO) conferred the IFFCO Sahitya Samman 2025 on eminent Hindi writer Maitreyi Pushpa for her outstanding contribution to Hindi literature, especially rural life and agrarian realities. The IFFCO Young Literary Award 2025 was presented to Ankita Jain for her book “Oh Re! Kisan.” Maitreyi Pushpa (born 30 Nov 1944, Aligarh) has a master’s in Hindi literature and is renowned for novels, short stories, plays, poetry, and women-centric writings. IFFCO Sahitya Samman, instituted in 2011, honors writers reflecting rural and agricultural life and carries a cash prize of ₹11 lakh, citation, and memento.

questions.question: Madhav Gadgil, who recently passed away, was associated with which field?
questions.explanation: Renowned Indian ecologist Madhav Dhananjaya Gadgil passed away on 7 January 2026 in Pune at the age of 82. He was the founder of the Centre for Ecological Sciences at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bengaluru. He chaired the Western Ghats Ecology Expert Panel (WGEEP) in 2010, known as the Gadgil Commission. He played a key role in establishing India’s first biosphere reserve, the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve (1986). He significantly contributed to the Biological Diversity Act, 2002 and People’s Biodiversity Registers. He received major honours including Padma Shri, Padma Bhushan, and United Nations Champions of the Earth Award (2024).

questions.question: Justice Revati Mohite Dere has become the first woman chief justice of which state?
questions.explanation: Justice Revati Mohite Dere was sworn in as Meghalaya’s first woman Chief Justice at Durbar Hall, Lok Bhavan, Shillong. The oath was administered by Governor Chandrashekhar H Vijayashankar. She succeeded Chief Justice Soumen Sen, who was transferred to Kerala High Court. Prior to this, Justice Dere served as a judge at the Bombay High Court. Her appointment was recommended by the Supreme Court collegium on 18 December 2025.

questions.question: Which Indian Army officer received the UN Secretary-General’s Award 2025 for her contribution to gender-inclusive peacekeeping?
questions.explanation: Major Swathi Shantha Kumar, an Indian Army officer from Bengaluru, received the UN Secretary-General’s Award 2025 for gender-inclusive peacekeeping. She is currently serving with the United Nations Mission in South Sudan (UNMISS). Her project, “Equal Partners, Lasting Peace,” strengthens UNMISS’s gender-inclusive approach. The award recognises initiatives promoting gender parity and gender-responsive peacekeeping. Her project was selected from UN-wide nominations and won the Gender Category with the highest votes globally.

questions.question: The Sukhatme National Award in Statistics is instituted by which ministry?

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation

answer.label: D
answer.text: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation

questions.explanation: Sukhatme National Award in Statistics is instituted by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI). It recognises outstanding lifetime contributions to statistics and improvement of official statistical systems. The award is given every alternate year since 2000 to eminent Indian statisticians aged 45 years and above. The award will be presented on Statistics Day, 29 June 2026.

questions.question: Which organisation received the SKOCH Award-2025 for its Cell Broadcast Solution?
questions.explanation: Recently, the Centre for Development of Telematics received the SKOCH Award 2025 at the 104th SKOCH Summit themed “Resourcing Viksit Bharat”. The award was given for its Cell Broadcast Solution, an indigenous disaster and emergency alert platform. The system integrates agencies like India Meteorological Department, Central Water Commission, Indian National Centre for Ocean Information Services, Defence Geo-informatics Research Establishment, and Forest Survey of India. It delivers near real-time, geo-targeted, multilingual disaster alerts through mobile networks with approval from State and National Disaster Management Authorities.

questions.question: Who is the author of the book Chalice of Ambrosia: Ram Janmabhoomi-Challenge and Response?
questions.explanation: Vice President Dr C. P. Radhakrishnan launched the book Chalice of Ambrosia: Ram Janmabhoomi–Challenge and Response in New Delhi. The book is authored by Surendra Kumar Pachauri, a former secretary to the Government of India. Dr Radhakrishnan stated that Ram Mandir at Ayodhya represents a defining moment in India’s civilisational journey, where faith, law, and democracy converged. The 2019 Supreme Court verdict fulfilled the aspirations of millions and highlighted the strength of Indian democracy.

questions.question: In India, the Asian Productivity Organization (APO) National Award is administered by which organisation?
questions.explanation: Bakul Limbasiya, founder of Bhathwari Technologies, received the APO National Award for productivity, innovation, and leadership. The award is instituted by the Asian Productivity Organization (APO) and administered in India by the National Productivity Council (NPC) under the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT). It is among India’s highest recognitions for advancing productivity and competitiveness. The award marks a milestone for India’s lab-grown diamond (LGD) industry. Limbasiya produced India’s first lab-grown diamond in 2004 and pioneered the sector since 1998.

questions.question: K. Pajanivel, who was recently conferred with the Padma Shri for traditional martial arts, belongs to which state/UT?
questions.explanation: K. Pajanivel of Puducherry has been awarded the Padma Shri for his contribution to traditional martial arts, specifically Silambam. Born on 30th January 1973 in Pooranankuppam, Puducherry, he trained under Master Rajaram. He has spent decades preserving, promoting, and popularising Silambam in India and abroad. He has previously received the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award (2023), Kalaimamani Award (2012), Best Youth Award (2004), and Silambam International Award (2002). He supports teaching Silambam in schools and aims to further promote it globally.

questions.question: Which port authority won the First Prize at the Swachhata Pakhwada Awards 2025?
questions.explanation: Paradip Port Authority (PPA) won First Prize at Swachhata Pakhwada Awards 2025 by the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways (MoPSW). Paradip Port Authority (PPA) was recognised for excellence in cleanliness, sustainability, and community participation. The achievement reflects commitment to Swachh Bharat Abhiyan and inclusive development. Paradip Port Authority (PPA) implemented three pillars: Jan Bhagidari, Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam, and Safai Mitra Suraksha.
